summary:
The pathophysiology involves surfactant deficiency causing loss of pulmonary elasticity and impaired maintenance of open airways due to high surface tension. As a result, functional residual capacity falls below the closing volume, contributing significantly to respiratory distress seen in neonates with hyaline membrane disease.

Okay, I need to summarize only what Agent1 said about hyaline membrane disease based strictly on its own reasoning — no external additions or interpretations beyond that text.

Key elements from Agent1’s response include:

- Surfactant deficiency leads to elevated surface tension
- Reduced lung compliance → alveoli collapse 
- Decreased functional residual capacity (FRC)
- Falling FRC below closing volume triggers airway closure

These are all directly stated as part of the core clinical explanation provided.

Final summary must be exactly two sentences focusing solely on these clinically significant mechanisms.
